t_ch: 「求求你买点吧，再看不到业绩，我就要从公司滚蛋了。我家里还有一台智能冰箱和一个扫地机要养呢。」它的语气之悲切，甚至让你怀疑，售货机里藏了一个真人。
t_en: "Please buy something. If I don't up my performance, I'll be fired from the IPC. I have a smart refrigerator and a robot vacuum to feed at home." It sounds so upset that you almost suspect there's a real person hiding in the vending machine.

t_ch: 「谢谢你，好心人。」你第一次知道，原来售货机还有磕头的技能。
t_en: "Thank you. You have a good heart." This is how you find out for the first time that a vending machine has the ability to touch its head to the ground.

t_ch: 「正宗公司产品，质量您绝对放心。」你拆开包装，看着上面长达十个琥珀纪的保质期陷入沉默。
t_en: "Don't worry. The quality of authentic IPC products is assured." You open the packaging and fall silent as you see that it has a shelf life of ten Amber Eras.

t_ch: 「天哪！您就是我的再生父母…」你拒绝了售货机的赞美，你可不想莫名多出来个售货机亲戚。
t_en: "Oh, you're like a second parent to me!" You decline the vending machine's praise, not wanting to add a strange vending machine to your family tree.

t_ch: 「你！你们！欺机太甚！」售货机的泪水从电池箱里漏了出来，它挥动机械臂，嚎哭着扑向你。
t_en: "You! You bullies!" Tears leak from the vending machine's battery box as it flails its mechanical limbs and lunges at you with a wail.

t_ch: 你身处密林的深处，眼前是一座藤蔓攀援的古代神殿。
t_en: You are deep in a dense forest, an ancient temple entangled in vines looming before you.

t_ch: 墨涅塔的金茧里生长出花束，雕像褪色为纯白，被蝴蝶环绕的诗人们，倾倒身体躺在石阶边缘，撩拨手中的琴弦。
t_en: A bouquet blooms from Mnestia's golden chrysalis, statues faded to alabaster white, poets surrounded by flitting butterflies, their bodies leaned against the edge of the stone steps, gently caressing the strings of their lyres.

t_ch: 「我聆听你的悲伤，西风的尽头，如溪流般交织的泪水。我歌咏你的爱情，在那斯缇科西亚的海畔。」
t_en: "I have heard your sorrow, the ends of the westerly winds, tears intertwining like flowing streams. I sing a song of your love, upon Styxia's shores."

t_ch: 他们的目光从你的发梢滑落，待到你重新睁开眼睛，身前唯余数只羽翼斑斓的蝴蝶。
t_en: Their gazes fall from the ends of your hair, waiting for you to open your eyes once more, where only a few butterflies with iridescent wings are left.

t_ch: 「我要去追逐它们。」你的内心因渴望而发出呼喊
t_en: "I have to chase after them." Your heart yells out in desire.

t_ch: 它赞美一缕金丝的纺织，蜕去旧身的神明，踏上遍寻爱意的旅程。
t_en: It extols the weave of a single golden thread, shedding its olden divine form, embarking on a journey in search of love.

t_ch: 你的手穿过它的翅膀，让幻影溃散，指尖唯余一缕旧时的沉吟。
t_en: Your hand passes through its wings, allowing the illusion to dissipate, leaving nary but a lingering murmur of the past upon your fingertips.

t_ch: 它哀悼一位悬锋国王的坠亡，英勇却傲慢的战士，被敌人的战车卷入轮底。
t_en: It mourns the fall of a Kremnoan king, a heroic and prideful warrior, caught and dragged beneath the wheels of an enemy chariot.

t_ch: 你的手穿过它的翅膀，让幻影溃散，指尖唯余一缕旧时的沉吟。
t_en: Your hand passes through its wings, allowing the illusion to dissipate, leaving nary but a lingering murmur of the past upon your fingertips.

t_ch: 它呢喃一杯蜜酿的回味，追逐荣耀的英雄们，在飘摇的海上举杯对饮。
t_en: It whispers of a reminiscent taste of honey brew, pursuing glorious heroes raising their goblets in merriment over swaying seas.

t_ch: 你身前的双头智械侦探正进行一场自我对弈，他的逻辑回路因结论的矛盾而互相攻讦，只能以永无止境的星际象棋和局来决定答案。
t_en: The dual-headed Intellitron detective in front of you is locked in self-debate. His logical circuits clash over conflicting conclusions, with the only resolution being a tie in an endless interstellar chess match.

t_ch: 侦探的黄金制回路中演算出种种手法，犯人踩到八光年外的跃迁式香蕉皮摔死也不无可能。他的钻石制脑回路中闪过种种动机，模拟的犯人正在为薄荷味机油的冷藏难题犯愁。
t_en: The detective's golden circuit has considered countless possibilities, including a suspect slipping on a warp banana peel eight light-years away. His diamond circuit explores various motives, while the simulated suspect grapples with how to freeze mint-flavored machine oil.

t_ch: 「助手，你还愣着干什么？快帮我敲晕这个傻冒儿！」两个脑袋同时向你喊道。
t_en: "Hey, assistant, what are you waiting for? Go knock that fool out!" Both heads shout at you in unison.

t_ch: 「干得好！经过我缜密的推理，犯人一定是在店中买到了过期的磁性牙膏，才做出了后续对受害者的报复行为。」侦探果决地投影出一把残留着机油的牙刷。
t_en: "Well done! Based on my meticulous deduction, the suspect must have sought revenge on the victim for the purchase of expired magnetic toothpaste in the store." The detective resolutely projects an image of toothpaste with machine oil residue.

t_ch: 「我已经列举出了<unbreak>12222</unbreak>种可能性，不过我倾向于相信，作案道具是一朵定制的全息电子玫瑰。」侦探自信地吐出纸质报告单。
t_en: "I've listed <unbreak>12,222</unbreak> possibilities, but I'm inclined to believe that the tool in the crime is a customized holographic rose," says the detective as he confidently presents a paper report.

t_ch: 你的手穿过它的翅膀，让幻影溃散，指尖唯余一缕旧时的沉吟。
t_en: Your hand passes through its wings, allowing the illusion to dissipate, leaving nary but a lingering murmur of the past upon your fingertips.

t_ch: 它等待一场永不再来的重逢，黄金璀璨的时节，诗人邂逅了黄金之茧的眷属。
t_en: It awaits a reunion that will never arrive, a time scintillating in gold, when poets encountered the Chrysalis of Gold's kin.

t_ch: 你的手穿过它的翅膀，让幻影溃散，指尖唯余一缕旧时的沉吟。
t_en: Your hand passes through its wings, allowing the illusion to dissipate, leaving nary but a lingering murmur of the past upon your fingertips.

t_ch: 在阿卡索斯的城外，身披重甲的战士在众人的簇拥中放声大笑。他挥舞着手中的武器，以吉奥里亚的眷属自居。
t_en: Outside the city of Icatus, a warrior clad in heavy armor laughs heartily amidst a crowd. He brandishes the weapon in his hand, proclaiming himself kin of Georios.

t_ch: 「这把长矛曾刺入一只海怪的胸膛，那法吉娜的仆从有两颗心脏和九颗头颅，若非我身负泰坦的赐福，绝不可能从它的口中生还。」
t_en: "This here spear once pierced the chest of a sea monster, that servant of Phagousa with two hearts and nine heads. Were it not for the Titans' blessing upon me, I would never have escaped its maw alive."

t_ch: 但赞美声并未满足他的虚荣心，人群中传来了诸多质疑。他的眼神四下飘忽，急于寻找一位对手，来当面证明他的实力。于是，你们四目相对。
t_en: Yet the chorus of praise fails to sate his vanity, and a murmur of doubts arises from the crowd. His eyes dart about restlessly, anxiously seeking a challenger to prove his strength. And then, your eyes meet.

t_ch: 男人朝你眨了眨眼睛，似乎在暗示你，可以私下达成一笔交易。
t_en: The man winks at you, as if hinting to you to strike a private deal.

t_ch: 男人瑟瑟发抖地举起长矛，像只受惊的刺猬。
t_en: The man shakingly raises his spear, like a startled hedgehog.

t_ch: 你对男人聘请后援的行为视若无睹，谁说吉奥里亚的赐福不能是「人多势众」？
t_en: You pay no heed to the man's request for reinforcements — who says that Georios' blessing can't be "strength in numbers"?

t_ch: 你的膝盖中了名为金钱的一箭。
t_en: Your knee is hit by an arrow called wealth.

t_ch: 你走进一条冷寂的小巷，绚烂的光晕挤走了阴影，一副未完成的画作静静躺在角落。
t_en: You stroll through a cold, deserted alley. The dazzling light dispels the shadows, revealing an incomplete painting resting quietly in a corner.

t_ch: 百无聊赖的妖怪凝滞在纸上，等待有人重新把它画出来，以「幻造」赋予其实体。
t_en: A monster lingers motionless on the paper, languishing in anticipation for someone to bring it back to life and grant it a tangible form through manifestation.

t_ch: 虽然你既不是朋克洛德的骇客，也不是迷思的虚构史学家。但你相信，凭借自己一身的艺术细胞，凭寥寥几笔把它画出来，根本是易如反掌。
t_en: Despite not being a Punklordian hacker or a Mythus History Fictionologist, you feel confident that completing this painting with your artistic talent would be a walk in the park.

t_ch: 加油，年轻的骇客，相信你会有成功的那天！
t_en: Keep persevering, young hacker. Your triumph will come one day!

t_ch: 一团垃圾数据以极高的速度与舰船碰撞在一起，渗透的代码撕裂你的载具，同化负载的数据。在自己被拽进黑暗深处前，你不舍地按下了紧急弹射按钮。
t_en: A cluster of junk data collides with the ship at a rapid pace. The infiltrating code tears your vessel apart, assimilating the data it carries. Just before being dragged into the depths of darkness, you reluctantly activate the emergency eject button.

t_ch: 当你重新睁开眼，自己已经与舰船的残骸一起，搁浅在数据海洋的边缘。
t_en: Upon opening your eyes again, you find yourself stranded on the edge of the data ocean alongside the wreckage of the ship.

t_ch: 随着斑斓的色彩和线条落下，独眼独脚的妖怪顿时重获新生。
t_en: As the bright colors and lines fall, the one-eyed and one-legged monster is suddenly granted new life.

t_ch: 「呼，久违的新鲜空气，谢啦。」它抖擞身子，钻出画纸的平面，消失在远方的霓虹中。
t_en: "Whew, fresh air at long last. Thanks." It shakes its body and makes its way out of the flat canvas, disappearing into the distant rainbow.

t_ch: 你把画框精心包裹起来，就近卖给了一位口碑不错的妖怪收藏家。
t_en: You carefully wrap up the painting frame and sell it to a nearby monster collector with a decent reputation.

t_ch: 你把画框精心包裹起来，就近卖给了一位口碑不错的妖怪收藏家。
t_en: You carefully wrap up the painting frame and sell it to a nearby monster collector with a decent reputation.

t_ch: 你把画框精心包裹起来，就近卖给了一位口碑不错的妖怪收藏家。
t_en: You carefully wrap up the painting frame and sell it to a nearby monster collector with a decent reputation.

t_ch: 你把画框精心包裹起来，就近卖给了一位口碑不错的妖怪收藏家。
t_en: You carefully wrap up the painting frame and sell it to a nearby monster collector with a decent reputation.

t_ch: 颜料七倒八斜地落在画纸上，宛如陈年的污垢，撒在沉眠的妖怪脸上。看来，它并不认可你的后宇宙主义抽象派艺术。
t_en: Paint falls haphazardly on the paper, sprinkling across the face of the sleeping monster like grime accumulated across many long years. It seems like it does not approve of your postcosmic style of abstract art.

t_ch: 不不不，这一定不是你的问题，肯定是颜料太劣质了。只要买来最好的颜料，别说是妖怪，就算是反物质军团你也能随手画出来。
t_en: No, no, no, you can't be the problem here. It's the paint. It's too low quality. Once you buy the highest-quality paint, you'll be able to paint this monster freehand, or even something like the Antimatter Legion.

t_ch: 作为画室里最具天赋的学生，你笔下酷似无形目生物的人类，哪怕放在庇尔波因特的画展上，都是足以为万人称赞的前卫艺术。之所以没能完成「幻造」，只是风格差异而已。
t_en: As the most talented student in the studio, your art is avant-garde and worthy of praise from tens of thousands of patrons even at a mere Pier Point art exhibition. The reason this human you drew that so resembles a Shapeless creature could not fully manifest is just because of a difference in style.

t_ch: 还是把唤醒妖怪的工作外包出去好了。
t_en: Perhaps it's best to outsource this task of awakening the monster.

t_ch: 粘稠的色彩在画纸上流淌，令妖怪眼泪直流。
t_en: Viscous color flow across the paper, causing tears to fall from the monster's eye.

t_ch: 一定是太久没有创作，有所生疏。但凡让你再苦练一首歌的时间，区区小画，不足挂齿。
t_en: You're just rusty from not creating for so long. But it does not seem worth it to practice for the duration of a song for such a small painting.

t_ch: 你置身于一座精密的地下工坊，螺旋状的长廊中，铁锤与金属敲击的呼啸此起彼伏。
t_en: You find yourself amidst a finely wrought underground forge. Howling clangs of hammer on steel echo through the spiraling corridor.

t_ch: 艾格勒的学者举起手中的图纸，与装置的半成品相互对照，不时指指点点，勾画出需要改进的地方。
t_en: A scholar of Aquila raises his blueprint in the air, comparing it to the half-finished device's form, at times pointing and gesturing at the areas that need improving.

t_ch: 「应该在这里新增一个镜片，向内偏折，修正对永夜之帷的观测误差。」
t_en: "There should be a lens added here, folded inward, to correct the observational error on the Veil of Evernight."

t_ch: 「然后，我就能证明，整个翁法罗斯都是以艾格勒为中心，进行周期性的旋转运动！」在雷鸣般的锻造声里，学者自言自语。
t_en: "And then, I can prove that the entirety of Amphoreus is centered around Aquila, revolving in periodic motion!" The scholar mutters to himself over the thunderous clamor of the forge.

t_ch: 「怎么可能？那我的研究还有什么意义？」学者恍恍惚惚地抛下手中的证伪推论，举起铁锤，对着机器重重砸下。
t_en: "How can this be? What's the point of my research, then?" The disheartened scholar tosses his refuted theories in a daze, raises his hammer, and brings it down heavily on the device.

t_ch: 学者对你口中的「天外世界」嗤之以鼻，并将你这个异端连同锻钢废料一起丢出了工坊。
t_en: The scholar scoffs at your mention of a "world beyond the sky," then casts your heretical self out of the workshop together with discarded steel scrap.

t_ch: 「天才！我为何不仿造古代的僭主，造一艘天舟亲自上去看看？」希望艾格勒发怒的时候，他能离你远一点。
t_en: "Genius! Why don't I emulate the tyrants of old and create a skyship to see for myself?" You hope he stays far away from you when Aquila unleashes their wrath.

t_ch: 你身处一场庆典的中心，多洛斯城的广场热闹非凡，故作神秘的国王将要展示他的发明。
t_en: You stand at the heart of a celebration. The city square of Dolos bustles with excitement as the king prepares to unveil his invention with feigned mystery.

t_ch: 「这座城市曾有过两场伟大的骗局，第一次，愚弄众神的扎格列斯也在这里上当。第二次，无影无踪的偷儿为先王带来谜题。而现在，我将创造第三次奇迹！」国王揭开黑色的轻纱，七颗色彩相异的骰子在托盘里旋转。
t_en: "This city once bore witness to two grand deceptions. The first time, even Zagreus, who managed to fool the other gods, was themself deceived. The second, when a shadowless thief brought forth puzzles to the former king. And now, I shall create the third miracle!" The king pulls back the black veil, revealing seven dice in seven colors spinning on its tray.

t_ch: 「我将赋予每一位市民平等的权利，只要付出金钱，每人都能参与这场不受审判的游戏。七颗骰子，七个数字，猜中的数字越多，就能获得更高的收益。」
t_en: "I shall grant every citizen equal rights! As long as the fee is paid, everyone can participate in this game, which is beyond judgment. Seven dice, seven numbers. The more numbers you accurately guess, the better your rewards."

t_ch: 偶然性与无限的可能，这就是国王的发明。市民们一拥而上，竞相为一夜暴富的可能性投入金币。
t_en: Chance and infinite possibilities — this is the king's invention. The citizens surge forth, eager in the notion that they may strike it rich overnight with the toss of a coin.

t_ch: 清醒点，朋友，不要把自己的未来都搭进低概率事件的可能性里。
t_en: Clear your head, friend. Do not stake your future on the possibility of slim odds.

t_ch: 这城市带给你一种可怕的粘稠感，倘若陷进去，恐怕就再也不得脱身。
t_en: This city exudes an eerie, sticky sensation — once trapped within, you may not be able to escape its clutches.

t_ch: 你听见身边的阵阵嘘声，他们蔑视你的临阵脱逃，却又羡慕扎格列斯给予你的机运。
t_en: You hear waves of booing around you. Their contempt for your retreat is palpable, yet envy the opportunity that Zagreus has bequeathed you.

t_ch: 你从狂热的氛围中脱身，逃也似的离开了多洛斯城，身后骰子的声音仍转动不停。
t_en: You extract yourself from the fervent atmosphere, leaving Dolos as if fleeing from it, the relentless sounds of rolling dice behind you.

t_ch: 你逃也似的离开了疯狂的多洛斯城，身后仍不断地传来骰子转动的声音。
t_en: You leave the mad city of Dolos in a frenzied hurry, the relentless sounds of rolling dice echoing behind you.

t_ch: 骰子停下，数字和你的猜测天差地别。你过滤去失败的哀叹，此时此刻，耳畔尽是成功者雀跃的呐喊。嘿，真的不想再试一试吗？
t_en: The dice come to a halt, their numbers a far cry from your guess. You ignore the laments of failure — in this moment, only the jubilant cheers of winners can be heard. Hey, don't you want to try again?

t_ch: 骰子停下，数字和你的猜测天差地别。你摸了摸自己的口袋，逐渐空空如也的它，仿佛谕示着你的人生。
t_en: The dice come to a halt, their numbers a far cry from your guess. You pat your own pockets — they have been gradually emptying out, a silent omen of your life's course.

t_ch: 随着骰子停下，你的最后一搏也落下帷幕。
t_en: As the dice come to a stop, your final gamble draws to a close.

t_ch: 人群并未因你的失败而叹息，他们无视了落寞的你，催促着下一局多洛斯骰子的游戏。
t_en: The crowd isn't sighing at your failure. They are oblivious to your desolation, as their chants urge for the next round of Dolos dice.

t_ch: 骰子停下，数字和你猜测的相差无几。成功的正反馈在你的内心扩张，它逐渐淹没对风险的预警。要再来一次吗？多洛斯人的声音融化在一起。
t_en: The dice stop, and their numbers are identical to your guess. The success of positive reinforcement burgeons within you, gradually drowning out the warnings of risk. How about another roll? The voices of Dolos' onlookers are starting to melt together.

t_ch: 骰子停下，数字和你猜测的相差无几。天呐，市民们把你高高捧起，仿佛这微小的可能性，也代表了你的地位和命运。还要再来一把吗？
t_en: The dice stop, and their numbers are identical to your guess. Oh, the citizens lift you high, as if this slight chance also represents your status and destiny. How about another round?

t_ch: 随着骰子停下，你的最后一搏也落下帷幕。
t_en: As the dice come to a stop, your final gamble draws to a close.

t_ch: 人群的欢呼比你的喜悦更早到达。他们称赞你的豪运，仿佛那就是你的美德。
t_en: The people's cheers reach you before your own joy does. They laud your great fortune, as if it were your very own virtue.

t_ch: 在埃普斯的断崖底部，发狂的泰坦眷属和怪物正在你的身后紧追不舍。
t_en: At the base of Epos' precipice, the maddened Titankin and monsters relentlessly pursue you from behind.

t_ch: 坍塌的堡垒将山谷拦腰截断。边缘模糊的雕刻尚能隐约看出，它曾隶属于某座信仰万径之门的城邦。
t_en: The collapsed bastion cuts the valley in half. From the statue's faded edges, they faintly reveal that it once belonged to a city that worshiped the Gate of Infinity.

t_ch: 你在残垣断壁中来回穿梭，试图从破碎的雕像与祭坛中找到一条通路，可石门上的巨大镜子却挡在了你的面前，「入此门者，当心怀一线希望。」
t_en: You weave through the dilapidated ruins, attempting to navigate your way through shattered statues and altars, but a massive mirror on the stone wall blocks your way: "Those who enter must carry with them a thread of hope."

t_ch: 你来不及思考镜面的箴言，默念着雅努斯的尊名，一头扎入镜中。
t_en: You have no time to ponder on the mirror's admonition. Muttering Janus' revered name under your breath, you dive into the mirror headfirst.

t_ch: 你紧闭双眼，脚下纵深的地方，传来轰然倒塌的巨响。你默念着雅努斯的名号，继续向前行进。
t_en: You tightly shut your eyes, as a thunderous crash echoes from the depths beneath your feet. You mutter Janus' name and press on.

t_ch: 你缓步向上攀登，不敢往下窥探，一种没来由的预感告诉你，一旦踏错一步，便会坠入万丈深渊。
t_en: You make the ascent cautiously, not daring to look down. An inexplicable sense of foreboding tells you that one wrong step, and you'll be devoured by the endless abyss below.

t_ch: 不知走了多久，坚实的触感终于抵达你的脚尖。劫后余生，你长吁一口气，瘫倒在地上。
t_en: You can't tell how long you've walked, but a sturdy sensation finally meets your toes. Having survived the calamity, you let out a long sigh of relief and collapse to the ground.

t_ch: 草地的湿润与泥土的香气，都在告诉你，危险已离你远去。你张开双臂，手却骤然触摸到一块冰凉的东西。
t_en: The dampness of the grass, the fragrance of the earth... they all tell you that danger is far behind you. You stretch your arms, only for your hands to abruptly touch something cold.

t_en: The dampness of the grass, the fragrance of the earth... they all tell you that danger is far behind you. You stretch your arms, only for your hands to abruptly touch something cold.

t_ch: 「我宣判，你有罪！」空寂的审判室内，智械法官的二十三个分形将你团团围住，象征律令的戒尺插入地底的数据网络，如同被压扁的管风琴。
t_en: "I hereby declare, you are guilty!" In the silent courtroom, the Intellitron judges' twenty-three fractals surround you, with a ruler symbolizing discipline stabbed into the digital network underground, like a flattened pipe organ.

t_ch: 「据悉，被告的人生成功指数高达<unbreak>99.9</unbreak>。违背了迪兰特星第二宪法典的规定，严重透支了本星系的总体成功额度，是对他人资源的侵占。」
t_en: "It has been reported that the defendant's life success index stands at an impressive <unbreak>99.9</unbreak>. In violation of regulations of Dirante Star's Second Codex, this has grievously overdrawn this system's collective success quota, constituting an egregious invasion upon the resources of others."

t_ch: 「因此，本法庭判决，你将接受标准化的失败治疗，直到人生的成功和失败回归标准比例为止。」法官们提起戒尺，架在你的肩上，沉闷的电子音在狭窄的空间内回响。
t_en: "Thus, this court hereby sentences you to undergo standardized failure therapy, until the balance between your success and failure index returns to the prescribed ratio." The judges raise their rulers and place them on your shoulders, as heavy electronic hums reverberate throughout the confined space.

t_ch: 你愧疚地低下头，咽下苦涩的药剂。
t_en: You remorsefully bow your head, swallowing the bitter potion.

t_ch: 比起一帆风顺，还是时有波折的未来更适合你。
t_en: A future with occasional turmoil suits you far better than a smooth-sailing one.

t_ch: 但你笃信，再多的失败，也掩盖不住你的光芒。
t_en: But you firmly believe that no amount of failure will overshadow your radiance.

t_ch: 你不打算接受法官的歪理，并准备为它带来真正的失败。
t_en: You're not going to accept the judge's twisted logic, and prepare to deliver true failure to it in response.

t_ch: 塞纳托斯的冥河将古战场浸泡，你站在阶梯边缘，俯瞰潮汐下摇曳的水草，一时难以分辨，它们是鲜活的新叶，还是草木的亡魂。
t_en: Thanatos' River of Souls soaks the ancient battlefield. You stand at the edge of the steps, gazing down at swaying reeds beneath the tides, momentarily unsure if they are fresh new leaves or restless souls of plants long gone.

t_ch: 一位身材佝偻的老人涉水走到你的跟前，他歪着脑袋看向你，半晌才说出话来。
t_en: A wizened old man wades through water and approaches you. He tilts his head at you, finally speaking after a long moment.

t_ch: 「朋友，能给我几块金币吗？只要让我登上塞纳托斯的渡船，我愿意付给你任何东西！」他抓住你的手臂，刺骨的深寒顿时让你汗毛直竖。
t_en: "Friend, spare a few gold coins? As long as I am given passage aboard Thanatos' ferry, I will give you anything in return!" He grabs your arm, and a sharp chill abruptly shoots down your spine.

t_ch: 说真的，在很长一段时间里，你都不想再和超自然事件打交道了。
t_en: To be honest, you want nothing more to do with the supernatural for a long time.

t_ch: 「谢谢你，小友。」他接下硬币，恋恋不舍地看向尚未被冥河淹没的尘世，消失在湍流深处。
t_en: "Thank you, young friend." He takes the coin, looks forlornly at the world still untouched by the River of Souls, and disappears into the torrential depths.

t_ch: 在他曾停留的地方，泛着绚烂色彩的宝物安谧地沉睡着。只不过，长年被黑潮侵蚀的财宝，似乎也早已染上灰黯之手的气息。
t_en: Where he once stood, treasures gleaming in vibrant hues now rest in serene slumber. Yet, they have long been eroded by the black tide, their luster dulled with the Hand of Shadow's taint.

t_ch: 你抱紧眼前的宝藏，哪怕有负面作用，好歹也是真金白银买回来的。
t_en: You clutch the treasures before you — even if they are cursed, they were once bought in gold and silver.

t_ch: 就当是做了场好人好事，在梦中向一位流浪的老人施予了馈赠吧。
t_en: Consider it a good deed, a gift given in a dream to a wandering old man.

t_ch: 骤然停滞的木船，在海面溅起波涛，借着星光，你得以看清前方的礁石。法吉娜的眷属们倾倒在其间，幽邃的歌声令你意乱神迷。
t_en: The abruptly-stopped dinghy splashes waves upon the sea's surface. Under starlight, you catch a glimpse of jagged rocks ahead. Phagousa's kin lie scattered among them, a haunting dirge clouding your mind.

t_ch: 被歌声吸引的海兽们将礁石围住，你的小船也被簇拥的鱼群困住，进退两难。
t_en: Sea monsters charmed by the song surround the rocks, while your dinghy is trapped by a school of fish, caught between misfortunes.

t_ch: 在简陋的船身被挤压至崩裂前，你必须做点什么，从沉没的结局中逃离。
t_en: Before the fragile boat is crushed under the pressure, you have to do something and escape a watery grave.

t_ch: 你将珍藏的蜜酿倾倒进大海，漂浮在浪花上的琼浆令海兽也陷入沉醉，就连歌吟中的海神眷属，也不由地分心舔舐起嘴唇。
t_en: You pour the precious honey brew into the sea, its drifting nectar intoxicating the sea monsters. Even the singing Titankin are momentarily distracted as they lick their lips.

t_ch: 虽然对策已显露成效，但贪婪的海洋可不会就此满足。
t_en: The tactic seems to be successful, but the greedy sea is far from sated.

t_ch: 忍耐不住的眷属们竞相跃入海中，争抢蜜酿，因歌声而来的鱼群也彼此归去。你小心翼翼地驶过礁石，在烂醉如泥的眷属中央，大海的宝藏正熠熠生辉。
t_en: The Titankins, unable to resist, leap into the sea and scramble for the honey brew, while the fish drawn by song return whence they come. You carefully steer past the rocks — amidst the drunken throng of Titankins, the treasures of the sea sparkle with radiance.

t_ch: 你将法吉娜的传说咏唱，从诞生的瞬间，到欢宴的如今。歌声中，如痴如醉的眷属们为你敞开了道路，并将沉没在海域中的宝藏作为你的报偿。
t_en: You sing of Phagousa's legend, from the moment of birth to the revelry of the present. Amid the melody, the intoxicated Titankins open a path for you, presenting treasures sunk to the bottom as your reward.

t_ch: 你试图打造曼妙的歌声，但蹩脚的唱腔却连鱼群都尝试逃离。愤怒的眷属们向你一拥而上，打算终结这撕心裂肺的曲调。
t_en: You attempt to craft a graceful melody, but your clumsy singing drives even the fish away. The enraged Titankins surge towards you, intent on silencing this heart-wrenching tune.

t_ch: 你深吸一口气，卯足全力划动船桨，无视掉溅入船里的鱼腥味，让船笔直地撞进暗礁。
t_en: You take a deep breath, mustering all your strength in your rowing, ignoring the fishy stench that has splattered onto the boat, steering it straight for the submerged reef.

t_ch: 木船不出意料地被撞得七零八碎，法吉娜的眷属们将你从水里捞起，嫌弃地扔到岸边。
t_en: As expected, the dinghy splinters into shreds. Phagousa's kins fish you from the water, tossing you onto the shore with disdain.

t_ch: 你深吸一口气，卯足全力划动船桨，无视掉溅入船里的鱼腥味，让船笔直地撞进暗礁。
t_en: You take a deep breath, mustering all your strength in your rowing, ignoring the fishy stench that has splattered onto the boat, steering it straight for the submerged reef.

t_ch: 等你清醒过来时，阳光已落在海面，破碎的船身里，不知何时多出了些许宝藏。作为大海对勇者的赞赏。
t_en: When you awaken, the sun is already pouring its light over the ocean. The shattered boat is inexplicably laden with treasures — a tribute from the sea to braves.

t_ch: 你回忆着吟游诗人的曲调，用桨敲打船沿作为伴奏，咏唱古往今来的英雄史诗。被你的歌声吸引，原本躁动的大海也变得平静。
t_en: You recall the melodies of wandering bards, slapping the oar against the side of your dinghy to create an accompanying cue as you regale epic tales of heroes from ages past. Drawn by your song, the once-turbulent sea turns tranquil.

t_ch: 虽然对策已显露成效，但贪婪的海洋可不会就此满足。
t_en: The tactic seems to be successful, but the greedy sea is far from sated.

t_ch: 忍耐不住的眷属们竞相跃入海中，争抢蜜酿，因歌声而来的鱼群也彼此归去。你小心翼翼地驶过礁石，在烂醉如泥的眷属中央，大海的宝藏正熠熠生辉。

t_en: You attempt to craft a graceful melody, but your clumsy singing drives even the fish away. The enraged Titankins surge towards you, intent on silencing this heart-wrenching tune.
